name: LA Urban Water Main Replacement
description: Replace approximately 1,800 linear feet of 12-inch cast-iron water main along Venice Boulevard between Overland Avenue and Beethoven Street in Los Angeles, CA 90066. Work includes open-cut trenching in the city right-of-way, reconnection of approximately 40 residential service laterals, new gate valves at each intersection, temporary service connections to maintain water supply during construction, and full pavement restoration. The corridor is a 4-lane arterial with on-street parking and a Class II bike lane. The project is funded entirely by LADWP capital funds.

name: Sacramento Stormwater Drainage Improvement Near Creek
description: Construct a new stormwater drainage improvement project in Sacramento, CA to address chronic flooding of a residential neighborhood adjacent to Arcade Creek, a tributary to the American River. The project involves installing approximately 600 linear feet of new 36-inch reinforced concrete pipe (RCP) storm drain, a new headwall outlet structure discharging to Arcade Creek at the base of the bank, regrading approximately 150 feet of creek bank, and installing riprap energy dissipation at the outfall. The project footprint is within the City of Sacramento ROW and on a narrow strip of Sacramento County Flood Control District easement along the creek. The creek is a jurisdictional waterway under both Clean Water Act Section 404 and California Fish and Game Code Section 1602.

name: San Jose Underground Electric Line Relocation
description: Relocate approximately 2,400 linear feet of existing overhead 12kV electric distribution line to underground conduit in San Jose, CA as part of a City-initiated street beautification and undergrounding program on Stevens Creek Boulevard between Winchester Boulevard and Saratoga Avenue. The project requires installation of new underground conduit banks (3-way schedule 40 PVC), new utility vaults at each intersection, coordination with PG&E for power transfer, removal of 18 existing wood poles shared with AT&T and Comcast fiber and coax attachments, and pavement trenching and restoration in a Caltrans-permitted corridor (Stevens Creek is a Caltrans-maintained state highway, Route 82). Project is partially funded with federal STP-Urban funds through the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority (VTA).
